headline: Kroger Names Emilee De Martino Executive Vice President and Chief People Officer
summary: The Kroger Co. (NYSE: KR) today announced Emilee De Martino will serve as its next Executive Vice President and Chief People Officer. De Martino succeeds Tim Massa, who announced he will be retiring September 18. De Martino plans to begin her time at Kroger by engaging with associates in stores, distribution centers, manufacturing facilities and offices across the country.

headline: The Dow Just Had Its Best First Half Since 2021, but This Jobs Number Is Flashing Yellow

headline: Americans are paying record prices for steak. Here's why demand isn't cracking
summary: Despite record beef prices, Americans aren't giving up steak. Consumers are treating beef as an affordable luxury and prioritizing it for special occasions.

headline: Here's why beef prices keep rising, and why consumers keep buying
summary: Beef prices hit record highs earlier this year as the U.S. cattle herd shrinks. But despite the elevated costs, consumers are still buying beef and steak in particular as they celebrate summer holidays. They're seeking not only value but also quality.

raw.headline: Here's why beef prices keep rising, and why consumers keep buying

headline: Kroger (KR) Is Buying Giant Eagle For $1.65 Billion To Expand Its Reach
summary: Kroger (NYSE:KR) has agreed to acquire supermarket chain Giant Eagle in a cash deal valued at US$1.65b. The transaction focuses on expanding Kroger’s store footprint and customer reach across the mid Atlantic and Midwest regions. The acquisition is expected to add to earnings per share in the medium term, subject to closing conditions and regulatory approvals. Kroger is one of the largest grocery retailers in the US, with a broad mix of supermarkets, fuel centers, and private label...

headline: Kroger (KR) Stock Looks Reasonable With Margins Still In Focus
summary: Kroger stock has given investors a solid 66.2% return over the past five years, yet its current checks suggest pricing that looks about in line with the market rather than clearly cheap or clearly expensive. A 66.2% gain over five years points to Kroger having rewarded patient shareholders, even though more recent returns have been weaker. Planned expansion through the Giant Eagle acquisition and growth in higher margin areas like retail media and eCommerce can support the long term story...

headline: Why Instacart (CART) Shares Are Plunging Today
summary: Shares of online grocery delivery platform Instacart (NASDAQ:CART) fell 5.7% in the afternoon session after Kroger's announced $1.65 billion acquisition of Giant Eagle sparked concerns about increasing consolidation in the grocery sector.
